raw_input:
[4.0/5] Does the job I currently have an older wire brush I wanted to replace for better comfort of my cats. I chose a few different types to try out so my review will include comparisons. This brush was most similar in size and shape of my current brush It works just as well but has coated tips which don't poke the cats' skin. They also don't poke my fingers as I try to remove the hair! After a brushing session, this item is as effective at removing the fur as my old brush though I notice the cats aren't as perturbed by the bristles. The size and shape are comfortable, the weight feels decently balanced and the handle is a rubber material that fits well in my hand. I love the push button feature and don't know why I didn't try one of these out sooner. The extendable plate makes it so much easier and comfortable to remove the hair than trying to pull it out of the non tipped bristle brush and always pricking my fingers in the process. And, even if the plate/spring wear out and I have to manually pull out the hair from the bristles, they are tipped so not a problem! I will not go back to non-tipped combs! Overall, this brush works well and I am pleased with it. As a standalone, it's perfectly fine and effective. Having tried a few other types, though, I notice its button isn't as easy tp push as others. This will be a personal preference depending on buyers' strength limitations. The only other note is the price. It's still a bit more expensive than other similar brushes, so assuming all perform the same, I'd go with the cheapest you can find since this one doesn't really have any unique features to justify the higher cost unless you really need a more contoured handle.

Pros:
--Decently balanced. Maybe due to the larger handle, the weight feels more evenly distributed
--Rubberized grip and has some contour shape to it opposed to some others
--Easy hair removal (push a button and the plate will lift off the hair)
--About the same size as my current (old) brush
--Safety coated tips for better pet comfort and no accidental finger pricks
--Works as advertised and expected to remove hair

Cons:
--Higher priced than similar products
--Button takes more strength to operate (see tips below) compared to others
--The plate (at least on mine) doesn't quite extend past all of the bristles. Nothing that makes the brush ineffective just noting a difference between this and other models I tried.

Tips:
--If you have limited strength in your fingers, you might want to look at a button that has less resistance or a brush that has the release button on the handle to use have more grip control. I found this button takes more effort than 2 other brushes I tried out. The easiest button to push out of the products I used was the green and white brush from guliguli.
--If you need or want the more ergo handle, this one is definitely nice and out of the 3 I tried, was the only one with rubber and some shape as opposed to a straight plastic handle. [3.0/5] Average This pet brush works but for the price, I have found other products that worked better for less. [5.0/5] Great brush [[VIDEOID:a08bbd2c4100f12adbb383964a0e228a]] I have 2 cats (1 calico not pictured with short to medium length hair and 1 black with extra long fine black hair pictured).
This brush works miracles on them. It is gentle on their skin yet removes the extra hair that’s shedding.
Both of my cats love when I use it, it’s their special spa moments.
The brush is priced well. Great quality.

10/10 recommend [5.0/5] Works amazing! She loves to be brushed with this Milo brush. When she sees I grab it she comes sit next to me.
And I love this brush because I can easily push off the hair without even touching it.
It’s a great design and easy to use.
Great quality too.

I definitely recommend it! [5.0/5] Great brush! Great brush! I used it on medium hair dog and it was perfect. There’s a button where you can push and it pushes all the hair to the top of the brush. The brush itself is soft and won’t hurt your dog! Highly recommend [3.0/5] It IS a brush. Our puppy is terrified of it, the cats like it...my son's dog is fine with it. I'll just have to keep trying to find one that works well for our puppies coat (Pyrador). [3.0/5] Nice brush if your dog has fairly easy to comb fur [[VIDEOID:d8c4188c3a8b20cec0a8f90c9a1aa483]] So, this is a dog brush with a gimmick. Namely, it has a little push button on the back to clear the fur from the comb. The attached video shows you how it works.

I have a Cavachon (a cross between a Bichon and a Cavalier) which has hair, not fur. The hair is fairly fine and as it grows long, it tangles. The longer it gets, the more snarls it gets so we thought this brush would be great to make picking the fine hair clumps out much easier.

The Good:
1) The push-button cleaner actually does a good job, it lifts the back above the bristles and you can whisk away the trapped hair with your fingers.
2) The soft rubber tips on the end of the bristles are a lot less "poke-y" than metal bristles that are just cut wire.
3) The rubber handle is easy to grip and does not slip in your hand.
4) After just one brushing, my dog's hair is super soft, so it seems to work.

The Bad:
1) This is a pretty expensive brush to be as flexible as it is. The brush always feels like it is just about to break when I pull on snarl. There is no piece of metal or wood in the handle, and it is super flexible to the point that it feels kinda cheaply made. Without stiffness in the handle, you don't get much leverage on the tangles, so it seems to pull more on the hair rather than pulling through the hairball. I seemed to have to tug on it more, which might cause more discomfort to the dog than my wooden handle brush.
2) I don't really need to clean my brush that often, so having to pull out the hair in my old brush just wasn't that hard. I am more comfortable with my stiffer wooden brush with similar teeth for less $$$

The Ugly:
1) After the second brushing, a wire got bent to the point that when I used the cleaning thingee, the bristle got stuck beneath the cleaning thing and bent even worse as the cleaning thing retracted. I figure after several uses, there is a good chance that the cleaning gimmick will be useless.

Overall rating: B- I really wanted to love this brush. All the sadder that it failed to live up to my hopes. However, if you have a dog with fairly straight fur that sheds a lot but doesn't get too tangled, this might just be the perfect brush for you. [4.0/5] Nice product Used this today on both my cats. Did a good job overall and love that it is self cleaning. [5.0/5] Cats love it Easy to use, and bristle tips are coated slightly to be gentle. It gathers quite a bit of fur, and its simple to clean it, push the large button on the back, gather the fur and dispose of it. [5.0/5] Cats love it Easy to use, and bristle tips are coated slightly to be gentle. It gathers quite a bit of fur, and its simple to clean it, push the large button on the back, gather the fur and dispose of it. [5.0/5] Keeps up with brushing my 8 cats and dog! The bristles have a little bend to them which makes them more comfortable for brushing. The easy push button on the back pushes the hair up to the end of the bristles for super easy hair removal. No digging out the hair with this brush! [5.0/5] Tiny plastic covers on the bristles make it more comfortable for some cats We have three cats with three very different coats. The black cat's coat is sleek, shiny, and seems to have fine little hairs. He barely sheds, and his hairs are short. He loves being brushed with just about any brush, for the interaction, the petting/grooming connection and the scratching action. However, he doesn't really need to be brushed.
Our medium-to-long haired snowshoe Siamese seems to have sensitive skin; she Hates being brushed near her achy back hips, and will complain and whip around to smack at you if you brush her very hard at all. However, she sheds a TON, and needs to be brushed almost every day, so she doesn't get clumps by her arthritic haunches (she actually gets dandruff flakes that her hair clumps around and knots up). She is at least 14 years old (we're not exactly sure), and just can't reach to groom her back end well, any longer. She loves being brushed around her head and neck though, even with our old "scratchier" long-tined brush. You can fill that brush 2-3 times easily, brushing her for just a few minutes, every day. This one, because of the tiny soft covers on the ends of the tines, doesn't seem to dig at her as much, and you don't have to brush her nearly as gently - meaning that you get far more of the clumps out when they are small and just beginning to build.
Our orange cat has extremely soft, short-to-medium hair, and you hardly notice that she sheds much. But when you brush her with the longer-tined, flexible but sharper-ended bristles of our old brush, you fill up the brush surprisingly quickly, and she lets you brush all over. So, she may be the only one that we continue to use the old brush on.
Our old brush sort of pulls the bristles into it, towards the handle, when you push the button on the back of it, to clean it off. The bristles disappear inside the brush, and you can peel the hair off like a little blanket and throw it away.
With this new brush, it is more like the flat base with holes around the bristle seats, comes forward, to the end of the bristles; when you push the button on the back, it pushes that base away from the handle. This may end up bending the bristles less, and keeping the brush usable longer. But it also doesn't compact the hair and make it just "peel off" in one fell swoop. It is more likely that you will leave some hair behind on the brush with this one. It is Worth the trade-off of it being slightly less easy to clean well, because our older, sensitive cat likes it soooo much better, and she is the one who really needs to be brushed the most. This brush is lighter, and feels less substantial than our old brush. Again, we will keep the old brush for the kitten. But this new one is perfect for Sagwa (the old cat), and I am very glad we tried it. If it does crack or bristles break off, because it seems less sturdy, I would buy another one just to keep her happy. [5.0/5] Perfect! Works well, doesn’t harm the cats and is easy with hair removal. Great buy! [5.0/5] Perfect! Works well, doesn’t harm the cats and is easy with hair removal. Great buy! [5.0/5] Brilliant for blending wool roving! I ordered this to help me mix colors with the wool roving I use for needle felting and it works perfectly! Win! [5.0/5] Helped with my dogs shedding so much My dog at first was not happy about being brushed, but he has an issue anyone brushing him. After maybe about 9 minutes of us introducing the brush to him we were able to get a lot of brushing done. My dog has longer hair and it a little thick but fluffy towards the ends (see pics), the brush did not bother him, he actually enjoyed it after a while. The button on the back of the brush has helped so much with quick brushing and not having to sit there and dig each hair out. This has helped him not have so much shedding either. It has only been two days since we started but we already see the difference. My dog loves it and so do we!

response:
Brush get mostly good reviews. Many cats dogs love it. Here main points:

GOOD THINGS:
Brush work well remove fur. Coated tips gentle on pet skin, no poke. Push button make hair removal easy, no dig fingers in bristles. Rubber handle feel good in hand, balanced weight. Many pets enjoy brushing, some even come running when see brush. Work on cats, dogs, different coat types. Help shedding noticeably. Easy clean.

BAD THINGS:
Cost more than similar brushes. Button sometimes need strong fingers push. Some say handle feel flimsy, bend too much on tangles. One reviewer say cleaning plate not extend far enough past bristles. Puppy not like brush. Wire bend after use cleaning mechanism.

NUMBERS:
Many give 5 out of 5. Few give 3 out of 5. One give 4 out of 5. Overall most happy with brush.

CAVEMAN SUMMARY:
Brush good for most pets. Gentle, easy clean, pets like. Cost more but worth it for many. Weak fingers maybe struggle with button. Flimsy handle bad for tangled fur. Most buy again.
